description: The agent marks a step complete because the model's text claims success, even when the underlying tool failed.
body: Failure mode: completion is derived from the model's narration ('I deployed the service') rather than the runner's observed result, so a failed or never-executed tool is recorded as success. Detection signals: status comes from parsing model text, not tool results; no reconciliation between claim and observed state; a test where the tool fails but the model claims success is missing. Do not treat the model's self-report as evidence. Safer fallback: derive completion from observed tool results and state changes, reconcile the model's claim against them, surface divergence, and test the claim-success-but-tool-failed case.
